Plugin.Name: msf
Plugin.Overview: A free and open-source, user-facing all-in-one management tool for DNS and proxy (mihomo / sing-box, in development), packaged for Unraid. msf installs a lightweight Utilities page entry plus a self-managed local service. The Unraid plugin page is only for enabling the rc service, setting the listen host/port, choosing the data directory, viewing status, and opening the separate msf WebUI. The full DNS/proxy management interface runs in that WebUI. After you complete the in-app setup wizard, msf can run MosDNS (DNS), Mihomo (proxy/TProxy), and the required nftables rules. Online MosDNS/Mihomo/Zashboard core downloads are verified against GitHub Release API asset SHA-256 digests before install; local uploads remain user-supplied files and are not marked as project-verified. Default sample subscriptions/nodes are inert placeholders only. Persistent data lives under /mnt/user/appdata/msf. Update and remove msf from the Unraid Plugins page (not via the in-app updater).
Plugin.Changes: ### msf 0.3.6 - Added animated MSF logo branding to the login page and README header. - Reworked README / README.en as entry pages and split Linux, fnOS FPK, Unraid PLG, and Docker install details into separate docs. - Clarified platform update/removal boundaries: Linux tarball/systemd uses `msf update` / `msf uninstall`; fnOS FPK and Unraid PLG use their platform managers; Docker remains experimental. - Fixed DNS port 53 setup preflight so permission/capability probe errors are warnings instead of false "unknown process occupied" blockers. - MosDNS startup now only pre-blocks on real port 53 occupancy or bind `EADDRINUSE`; non-occupancy probe warnings allow MosDNS to attempt startup and report the real runtime error. - Normalized service CPU usage by available vCPU/logical CPU capacity to avoid dashboard values above 100% on PVE / multi-vCPU environments.
